notes:
EX Hidden Legends (ex5, released June 2004), ex-era English booster: 9 cards/pack, 36 packs/box, NO ETB (vintage). Per-pack structure (Bulbapedia Booster pack TCG): ~4 common, ~3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo (common/uncommon/rare filler with set-logo stamp), 1 rare-or-better slot. Set composition (Bulbapedia / CardMavin / given buckets): 32 common, 33 uncommon, 12 rare, 15 rare-holo, 9 ex (Rare Holo ex), 1 secret-rare (Groudon 102/101 = "Rare Secret", a gold-bordered ★ card numbered beyond the 101-card base set).

GOLD STAR: confirmed ABSENT. Gold Star cards debuted in the NEXT set, EX Team Rocket Returns; PokeAsset's list of the 10 Gold-Star sets does not include Hidden Legends. The available buckets also lack a gold-star key, consistent with this. So the top of the rare slot is ex (9 cards) and the single secret rare.

Rare-slot modeling (sums to 1.0): ex 0.069 (~2.5 ex/box; mid-ex-era falls between Power Keepers' generous ~1:8-12 and Holon Phantoms' stingy ~1:36 — community era estimate ~2-3 ex per 36-pack box); secret-rare 0.014 (~0.5/box; ex-era single secret rares run ~1 per 2 boxes, ~1:72 packs era average); rare-holo 0.397 (~14.3/box; "holo rare ~1 in 3 packs" modern baseline scaled up for this set's larger 15-card holo pool); rare 0.52 (~18.7/box non-holo rare, the default rare). Sum of rare-or-better slot = 36/box, matching the "12+ rares-or-better per box" guideline.

Reverse-holo slot distribution mapped to the set's common/uncommon/rare buckets proportional to pool sizes (32 commons, 33 uncommons, 27 rare+rare-holo): common 0.40, uncommon 0.41, rare 0.19 (rare reverses fold rare+rare-holo printings into the 'rare' bucket since reverse-holos exist of all three).

confidence=low: no per-set published pull-rate study exists for ex-era; figures are era-average community estimates applied to this set's known composition. shipEv=true (this is a real booster product with a standard ex-era structure). Vintage sealed box carries a large collector premium, so EV/pack will sit far below the sealed box price — expected, not a sign the rates are too low.
